The fact that loan duration is longer, as longer loan durations increase the likelihood of credit risk, because longer loan durations increase the likelihood of credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [1]
The fact that absence of a checking account, as the absence of a checking account is associated with a lower estimated credit risk, because the absence of a checking account is associated with a lower estimated credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [2]
The fact that negative checking account balance, as a negative checking account balance is positively associated with an increased probability of credit default, because a negative checking account balance is positively associated with an increased probability of credit default, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
